target_hardware: Lenovo V14-ADA first, generic x86_64 laptops later
architecture: custom Linux distribution with KDE Plasma internals and Zenith-owned UX
win_condition: Boot a Linux-based ZenithOS image composed from Zenith apt packages into a clean modern Wayland desktop with apt, Flatpak, first-party Zenith apps, self-hosted image-building tools, and a projected installed footprint under 30GB.

workstation.description: Linux kernel, systemd, apt/dpkg, Flatpak, KWin/KDE Plasma internals, GTK/libadwaita, and ZenithShell.

kernel_lab.description: Existing custom Limine kernel retained for OS internals learning and experiments.

kernel.reason: Real desktop internals, hardware support, firmware, graphics, power management, and drivers require Linux interfaces.

init_system.reason: KDE Plasma sessions, logind, journald, udev, device policy, and desktop services expect systemd semantics.

package_manager.reason: The user explicitly wants apt, and Debian-compatible packages give a stable system update substrate.

app_runtime.reason: Flatpak provides modern app distribution and sandboxing without replacing apt for the system.

display_server.reason: This gives premium compositor/session behavior while allowing a custom Zenith panel layout.

toolkit.reason: First-party apps are built using GTK4/libadwaita for consistency and runs natively under KDE Plasma.

audio.reason: Modern desktop audio, screen capture, Bluetooth audio, and portals depend on this stack.

network.reason: Settings and shell status need a mature network control API.

security.choice: polkit, AppArmor profiles where available, signed apt metadata, Flatpak portals
security.reason: Privilege boundaries should use existing Linux desktop security interfaces.

rollback.reason: Snapshot rollback is smaller and simpler than A/B partitions inside the 30GB target.

self_hosting.choice: ZenithOS ISO includes zenith-build, source manifests, and Linux image tools
self_hosting.reason: The OS should rebuild its own rootfs and ISO from inside the live or installed system after the first seed image exists.

release_engineering.choice: package build -> apt repository -> image compose
release_engineering.reason: Large distributions build packages in clean environments, publish repositories, and compose images from repositories instead of copying app files directly into an image.

feature_order.choice: desktop and first-party apps before installer polish
feature_order.reason: The OS must feel usable before installation and self-hosting become the main milestone.

verification_gate.storage: projected installed footprint must stay under 30GB
verification_gate.boot_test: image boots to SDDM or ZenithShell session on Wayland
verification_gate.package_test: apt update, apt install dry-run, and Flatpak remote verification pass inside ZenithOS
verification_gate.self_host_test: boot the ISO, run zenith-build check, then build a new rootfs from /usr/src/zenithos
verification_gate.kernel_lab: old make run path remains available as kernel-lab-run
